text.title: django-models Failure Modes
text.summary: Seven Django model antipatterns the spec must forbid or detect.

antipattern.symptom: New model lacks uid + created_at + updated_at. API responses leak integer id. Audit script flags inconsistency vs sibling models.
antipattern.root-cause: Developer wrote `class Foo(models.Model)` instead of `class Foo(BaseModel)`. The project BaseModel is bypassed; common fields are not inherited.
antipattern.fix: Replace base to extend BaseModel / SoftDeletableModel. Audit grep -r "models.Model" apps/*/models.py and flag. Spec rule r1-base-inheritance forbids direct inheritance for domain models.

antipattern.symptom: Invalid data lands in the DB; clean() validators are documented but never fire. Integrity errors appear hours later in downstream queries.
antipattern.root-cause: Service code calls obj.save() without obj.full_clean(). Django's save() does NOT call full_clean(); only ModelForm and Django Admin do.
antipattern.fix: Wrap saves in services: obj.full_clean(); obj.save(). Spec field full_clean_in_service=true is the contract.

antipattern.symptom: API response includes both `id` (integer) and `uid` (UUID). External consumers store the integer; an internal table-rebuild changes ids and breaks consumers.
antipattern.root-cause: Serializer's Meta.fields lists both `id` and `uid`. Defeats the purpose of the dual-field pattern.
antipattern.fix: Exclude `id` from serializers; expose only `uid`. Set lookup_field = 'uid' on viewsets. Spec [[django-api]] enforces this at the API layer.

antipattern.symptom: Migration applies in dev (SQLite); CI migration on Postgres fails OR vice-versa. Different test environments produce different schema.
antipattern.root-cause: GeneratedField(db_persist=True) or partial-condition UniqueConstraint used without pinning the DB engine. SQLite + MySQL support is partial/different.
antipattern.fix: Pin db_engine=postgresql when using partial indexes / GeneratedField. Spec rule rejects partial conditions on non-PostgreSQL.

antipattern.symptom: Squashed migration silently drops a unique=True constraint and re-adds it without backfilling; production rows that violate the constraint cannot be saved after deploy.
antipattern.root-cause: `python manage.py squashmigrations` was run without human review of the output. Squashed file lost a partial-index constraint that the original had.
antipattern.fix: Every migration file (including squashed) must be reviewed by a human before `migrate`. Lint rule blocks auto-merging migration PRs without an explicit review approval.

antipattern.symptom: Write latency on the orders table doubles. INSERT plans show 14 index updates per row.
antipattern.root-cause: db_index=True was added to every field "for performance". Each index multiplies write cost; many of them are never used in any read query.
antipattern.fix: Spec rule r8-index-justification: every db_index requires a reason ≥ 10 chars referencing a production filter/order_by/distinct path. Audit existing indexes against pg_stat_user_indexes.

antipattern.symptom: PR adds a spurious AlterField migration that does nothing semantically; reviewers waste time understanding it.
antipattern.root-cause: Developer reordered model fields "to clean up". Django detects the new order and emits a no-op AlterField migration.
antipattern.fix: Lock field order via code review. Do not reorder for cosmetic reasons. If field order must change, do it in one focused PR with a hand-edited migration.
